name: Whiteparish
overview: Whiteparish is a village and civil parish on the A27 about 7.5 miles south-east of Salisbury, sitting close to the Hampshire county border and partly within the New Forest National Park landscape. With a population of around 1,500, it has comprehensive amenities for its size including a community-run shop, primary school, surgery and a Grade II listed church (All Saints). The parish, recorded in Domesday as Frustfield, includes the hamlets of Cowesfield Green and Newton.
character: A pretty, active village with notably strong community spirit, where residents help fund amenities such as the Memorial Centre and playground through a village lottery.
amenities: Community-run village shop and Post Office
Two to three pubs including The Kings Head
All Saints Church (Grade II listed) and a second church
Recreation grounds with football and cricket pitches, tennis courts, MUGA and play area

schools.name: Whiteparish All Saints CofE Primary School
Secondary schools in Salisbury and Romsey area

prices.summary: A popular village with amenities within walking distance; period cottages and family homes (some bespoke builds by local firms) in a New Forest-fringe setting. See Rightmove for current sold-price detail.

thingsToDo: Walks along the River Blackwater and into the New Forest fringe
Nearby Newhouse Estate and Dean Station heritage area
pros: Comprehensive amenities for a village its size (shop, school, surgery)
Edge of the New Forest National Park
Reasonable drive to both Salisbury and Romsey
cons: Sits directly on the busy A27
No railway station in the village (nearest at Dean, limited service)
Car-dependent for secondary schooling and bigger shops
whoItSuits: Families and community-minded buyers wanting a well-served village between Salisbury and the New Forest.
nearestStation: Dean (~2.3 miles, limited service); Salisbury for main services
primarySupermarket: Community village shop; Waitrose in Romsey

sources.label: Whiteparish Post Office and Village Shop - See Around Britain
